appraisal_events.evidence: Avec une feinte humilité... une vanité si profonde... ses yeux jetaient... des regards pétillants de joie sous l'ironie desquels rougit le pauvre historien; « je ne suis que de l'Union et du Jockey... vous n'êtes pas du Jockey, monsieur ? »
appraisal_events.explanation: The duke displays his society affiliations to put down the historian and imposes himself in the conversation; the narrator ironically underscores his vanity, but the duke’s local domination is clear.

status_effects.explanation: He gains the upper hand in the exchange by humiliating his interlocutor and winning over the audience through his superiority of tone and of networks.
